spans.text: His awful arm revealed of yore
starting from your long cold rest
And once again with plumed pride
with sails all swelling white , Britain's proud fleet , to many a joyful cry , Ride over the rolling surge in awful sovereignty
That the great sun with haughty smile defied ; And Avarice
over the dying and the dead The victor's brazen wheels with gory axle rolled
They , that like a wasteful flame
the cry Of nations in distress
over the tranquil tide With beauteous bend
the Nile's affrighted shore , Where , from his oozy bed , The cowering crocodile hath raised his head
the East a glimmering freckle streaks , Slow over the shadowy wave the grey dawn breaks
But Britain's floating bulwarks , with serene And silent pomp , amid the deathful scene
noise of human things for ages hid
thy pride and pomp of yore
with triumph , and with hope again , The shouts of joy that swell from thy forsaken main
at whose command Deep darkness shades a mourning land
No more should meek-eyed Piety Outcast
No more should meek-eyed Piety Outcast
No more should meek-eyed Piety Outcast
the farthest fountains of the Nile
her awful lore , yet soft consoling voice
